signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The RBA's 4.10% rate with a hike bias supports the Aussie, while the +35bp carry advantage over the Fed's 3.75% rate adds to the upside pressure. The next RBA meeting on May 5 will be the catalyst to resolve this setup.

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Disadvantage
signals.text: JPY's 1W momentum (+1.08%) runs against the 290bp US-JGB yield gap, setting up a yield-driven unwind. The JPY's recent strength, with a 1W average of +1.081% (n=7 pairs), contrasts with the USD's weakness, averaging -0.872% (n=7 pairs), resulting in a G8 spread of 1.953%. The next BoJ meeting will be the catalyst to resolve this yield disadvantage-driven setup.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The tariff escalation acts as a catalyst for the EUR/USD move, driven by the mechanism of trade tensions impacting the euro. The next ECB meeting on April 30 will influence the euro's direction.
evidence: Trump tariff escalation on EU autos

signals.text: The current regime is mixed, with the VIX at 17.0 and the SPX up 0.29%, indicating a neutral market environment. The AUD/USD and AUD/NZD pairs show extreme short positioning among retail traders, with 90% short, which could lead to a contrarian reversal. The G8 currency strength scorecard shows the JPY as the leader, with a 1W average of +1.081%, while the USD is the laggard, with a 1W average of -0.872%.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575, with the BoE holding its rate at 3.75% and a market bias towards a hold. The GBP COT long position of +32.6k (67.3% long) supports the pound, but the lack of a rate hike may lead to a unwind of long positions. The next BoE meeting on April 30 will provide clarity on the BoE's stance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ's forward bias points to a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a sharp unwind, likely to pressure the pair higher. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is a key catalyst as the key catalyst.
